checks.description: Maintenance Windows let admins schedule reprovisioning, image updates, and disruptive changes during agreed business-quiet hours rather than ad-hoc.
checks.bestPractice: Define and assign a maintenance-window schedule per persona (for example Sunday 02:00 local). Include the schedule in user comms.
checks.impact: Without maintenance windows, image and configuration changes interrupt active users, generating help-desk tickets and SLA impact.
checks.remediation: Create a maintenance-window policy in Intune, scope to provisioning policies, validate timezone alignment, and publish to end users.

checks.name: RDP Multipath Connection Optimization
checks.description: RDP Multipath enables the Windows App to use multiple network paths simultaneously for higher throughput and resilience to single-link congestion.
checks.bestPractice: Where dual-homed users (corporate Wi-Fi + LTE/5G) exist, enable RDP Multipath so the session uses the best-available path and survives single-link drops.
checks.impact: Without Multipath, single-link congestion or interface flap drops the session even when an alternate working path exists.
checks.remediation: Enable RDP Multipath via Intune Settings Catalog on the targeted user devices. Validate with a test user on a dual-homed laptop.

checks.name: ANC Domain Credential Lifecycle
checks.description: Hybrid-joined ANCs use a service-account credential for domain-join. That credential must be rotated periodically and never shared with humans.
checks.bestPractice: Use a dedicated, least-privilege AD service account with delegated rights only on the target OU. Store the credential in a vault, rotate every 90-180 days, and update the ANC each time.
checks.impact: A compromised or stale ANC service account can break new provisioning across the entire ANC and provides AD foothold to attackers.
checks.remediation: Document the rotation cadence, store the credential in Azure Key Vault, and update the ANC via the Intune admin center after each rotation.

checks.name: Microsoft Purview Customer Lockbox
checks.description: Customer Lockbox requires explicit customer approval before Microsoft engineers can access tenant data during a support incident. Verify it is enabled for Windows 365.
checks.bestPractice: Enable Customer Lockbox tenant-wide and define an approver group so any Microsoft access request to Cloud PC data triggers a documented approval workflow.
checks.impact: Without Lockbox, Microsoft can access tenant data during a support case without per-incident customer approval, weakening regulatory and audit posture.
checks.remediation: In Microsoft 365 Admin Center > Org Settings > Security & Privacy, enable Customer Lockbox. Configure an approver group and rehearse the approval flow.

checks.name: Microsoft Purview Customer Key for Windows 365
checks.description: Microsoft Purview Customer Key adds a customer-controlled root of encryption above Microsoft-managed service encryption for supported Windows 365 surfaces.
checks.bestPractice: For regulated workloads, complement Azure CMK on Cloud PC disks with Microsoft Purview Customer Key for service-tier encryption.
checks.impact: Without Customer Key, Microsoft-managed service encryption keys are the only protection for some service-tier data, which may not satisfy regulator requirements.
checks.remediation: Provision keys in Azure Key Vault Premium / Managed HSM, register them through Purview, and assign the Customer Key configuration to Windows 365 if eligible.

checks.name: Windows Cloud Keyboard Input Protection (Preview)
checks.description: Windows Cloud Keyboard Input Protection (in preview) encrypts keystrokes between the Windows App client and the Cloud PC at the kernel level, mitigating local key-logger malware on the endpoint. Preview features may change before GA; track availability before broad rollout.
checks.bestPractice: Enable Cloud Input Protection for high-sensitivity personas (privileged operators, finance, R&D) where local endpoints may be exposed to commodity malware.
checks.impact: Without Cloud Input Protection, a key-logger on the local endpoint can capture credentials and data typed into the Cloud PC session.
checks.remediation: Enable the Cloud Input Protection policy via Intune Settings Catalog and assign to the in-scope user groups. Validate on a pilot device.

checks.name: Restrict Office 365 Access to Cloud PCs
checks.description: A Conditional Access policy can restrict access to Microsoft 365 apps so that data only opens inside a compliant Cloud PC, preventing copy-out to unmanaged endpoints.
checks.bestPractice: For data-sensitive personas, scope a Conditional Access policy that requires the Cloud PC compliant device for Office 365 access, blocking sign-in from unmanaged endpoints.
checks.impact: Without this scoping, users can open the same Office 365 data on unmanaged BYOD devices, defeating the data-isolation value of Cloud PCs.
checks.remediation: Build a Conditional Access policy targeting Office 365 cloud apps, scoped to the user group, requiring device compliance and the Cloud PC platform. Pilot, then enforce.

checks.name: Place Cloud PC Under Review (Forensic Hold)
checks.description: Place Cloud PC Under Review snapshots a Cloud PC's disk and isolates it for digital-forensics investigation while keeping the user productive on a fresh provisioned PC.
checks.bestPractice: Document an Under Review procedure as part of the IR playbook so that on suspicion of compromise, the affected Cloud PC is preserved for forensics within minutes.
checks.impact: Without a documented procedure, IR teams reprovision or wipe the affected Cloud PC and destroy forensic evidence.
checks.remediation: Add the Under Review action to the IR runbook, define who is authorized to invoke it, and rehearse via a tabletop annually.

checks.name: External Identities (B2B Guest) Cloud PCs
checks.description: Windows 365 supports provisioning Cloud PCs to external (B2B guest) identities for contractors, vendors, and merger scenarios. Verify whether the policy posture is documented.
checks.bestPractice: Decide whether external identities are in-scope. If so, scope a dedicated provisioning policy, image, and CA policy with stricter session controls.
checks.impact: Without an explicit posture, ad-hoc B2B Cloud PC provisioning may bypass standard hardening or violate licensing rules.
checks.remediation: Document the position. If allowed, build a dedicated B2B provisioning policy with appropriate CA, DLP, and image restrictions.

checks.name: Passwordless Credential Strategy
checks.description: Microsoft documents a credential-choice guide for Cloud PC sign-in covering Windows Hello for Business, FIDO2 keys, certificate-based auth, and Microsoft Authenticator.
checks.bestPractice: Pick a primary passwordless credential per persona (for example WHfB for staff, FIDO2 for admins) and document fallback. Avoid mixing too many factors per persona.
checks.impact: Inconsistent credential choices increase support cost, weaken phishing resistance, and complicate audit.
checks.remediation: Document the per-persona credential matrix, configure Authentication Methods policy in Entra accordingly, and roll out to pilot then production.

checks.name: Convert Image to Generation 2
checks.description: Generation 2 VMs are required for newer Windows 365 SKUs and offer Secure Boot and vTPM. Verify any custom images are Gen 2.
checks.bestPractice: Standardize on Generation 2 for all custom images. Convert any remaining Gen 1 images using the documented procedure before they block a SKU change.
checks.impact: Gen 1 images cannot use Secure Boot / vTPM and may be incompatible with newer Cloud PC SKUs, blocking modernization.
checks.remediation: Identify Gen 1 images in the Compute gallery, run the conversion procedure, validate provisioning, and retire the Gen 1 versions.

checks.name: Cisco Webex / Zoom Optimization
checks.description: Cisco Webex VDI and Zoom VDI plugins offload media processing to the local endpoint, similar to Teams VDI optimization. Verify they are deployed where users rely on those apps.
checks.bestPractice: For each non-Teams real-time comms app in production use, deploy the vendor VDI optimization plugin (Webex VDI, Zoom VDI, etc.) to the Cloud PC image and the local endpoint.
checks.impact: Without optimization, audio/video runs entirely inside the Cloud PC, consuming CPU and degrading call quality vs an optimized Teams baseline.
checks.remediation: Add the vendor VDI plugin to the image build (Webex VDI / Zoom VDI). Deploy the matching client to local endpoints via Intune. Validate call quality with a pilot.

checks.name: Cloud PC Recommendations Engine Review
checks.description: Microsoft's built-in Cloud PC Recommendations engine surfaces sizing, image, network, and policy recommendations specific to the tenant. Confirm the recommendations are reviewed and triaged on a cadence.
checks.bestPractice: Review the Cloud PC Recommendations report monthly. Triage each item to Accept / Defer / Reject and record the decision.
checks.impact: Untriaged recommendations mean the tenant is not benefitting from Microsoft's own data-driven guidance and may carry avoidable cost or risk.
checks.remediation: Add the review to the monthly operational rhythm. Capture triage decisions in the change-management system.

checks.name: Copilot in Intune for Windows 365
checks.description: Copilot in Intune can answer Windows 365 administrative questions, summarize tenant state, and accelerate troubleshooting. Confirm whether it is enabled and admins are trained.
checks.bestPractice: Enable Copilot in Intune for the Windows 365 admin team and document approved use cases (troubleshooting, change-impact analysis, onboarding).
checks.impact: Without Copilot, Windows 365 admins spend more time on documentation lookups and routine triage.
checks.remediation: Acquire required Copilot licensing, enable in Intune, and run a short enablement session with the W365 ops team.

checks.name: Partner Connectors (Citrix HDX Plus / Omnissa / HP Anyware)
checks.description: Microsoft supports third-party connectors (Citrix HDX Plus for Windows 365, Omnissa Horizon, HP Anyware) that layer alternative remoting protocols on top of Cloud PCs. Verify whether any are in use and governed.
checks.bestPractice: Document whether partner connectors are in scope. If yes, capture vendor support boundaries, licensing model, and operational ownership.
checks.impact: Unmanaged partner connectors create overlapping support boundaries and may bypass Microsoft baseline controls.
checks.remediation: Inventory deployed connectors. For each, document scope, licensing, support contacts, and integration with Intune controls.

checks.name: Cloud PC Monitoring (Preview)
checks.description: Cloud PC Monitoring (preview) is a dedicated near-real-time troubleshooting surface in Intune for Cloud PC connection, sign-in, and health signals. Confirm whether the team has opted in and integrated it into the support workflow.
checks.bestPractice: Opt in to Cloud PC Monitoring (preview), grant access to L1/L2 support, and document the triage steps that use it before traditional log queries.
checks.impact: Without it, support teams rely on slower batch reports and Endpoint Analytics aggregations to triage live user issues.
checks.remediation: Enable Cloud PC Monitoring in Intune, scope access via RBAC, and add the workflow to the support runbook. Track preview status until GA.

checks.name: Cloud PC Configurations (Preview)
checks.description: Cloud PC Configurations (preview) bundles provisioning policy, user settings, image, and network into a single reusable configuration object to simplify governance at scale.
checks.bestPractice: Evaluate Cloud PC Configurations (preview) for tenants with many provisioning policies. Standardize on configurations to reduce drift across personas.
checks.impact: Without configurations, large estates accumulate near-duplicate provisioning policies that are hard to keep aligned.
checks.remediation: Pilot a Cloud PC Configuration on one persona, validate, then migrate other personas. Document preview status and rollback path.

checks.name: AI-Enabled Cloud PC (Preview)
checks.description: AI-enabled Cloud PCs (preview) bundle Copilot+ PC capabilities (Recall, on-device AI) into the Cloud PC. Verify whether the persona requires AI features and whether preview rollout is approved.
checks.bestPractice: For knowledge-worker personas with approved AI use cases, pilot AI-enabled Cloud PCs and pair with Manage AI-enabled features policies (Recall on/off, etc.).
checks.impact: Without an explicit AI posture, Copilot/Recall surfaces may either be unavailable to users who need them or enabled without governance.
checks.remediation: Define the AI persona scope. Pilot AI-enabled Cloud PCs and configure Manage AI-enabled features policies to control Recall and other AI surfaces.

checks.name: Windows App Settings Policy (Preview)
checks.description: Windows App Settings (preview) lets admins centrally manage Windows App client behavior (default views, redirection defaults, sign-in modes) via Intune.
checks.bestPractice: Use Windows App Settings (preview) to enforce a consistent client experience and tested redirection defaults rather than relying on per-user choice.
checks.impact: Without centralized control, Windows App configuration drifts across endpoints and complicates support.
checks.remediation: Create a Windows App Settings policy in Intune, scope to test users, validate, then expand. Track preview status.

checks.name: Remote Connection Experience Policy (Preview)
checks.description: Remote Connection Experience (preview) controls session-level UX defaults (graphics profile, redirection toggles) per provisioning policy or persona.
checks.bestPractice: Define Remote Connection Experience profiles per persona (for example High-Quality for designers, Bandwidth-Saver for branch staff).
checks.impact: Without per-persona tuning, all users get the same defaults, leading to either poor experience or unnecessary bandwidth use.
checks.remediation: Create Remote Connection Experience policies in Intune, assign per persona, and validate with pilot users.

checks.name: Power Platform Connector for Windows 365 (Preview)
checks.description: The Windows 365 Power Platform connector (preview) enables Power Automate flows for Cloud PC lifecycle events (provision, reprovision, resize) without writing Graph code.
checks.bestPractice: For low-code automation teams, pilot the Power Platform connector to automate joiner/mover/leaver and resize approvals.
checks.impact: Without it, lifecycle automation must be built in PowerShell/Graph and is harder for business analysts to own.
checks.remediation: Install the connector in the Power Platform environment, scope a service principal with least-privilege Cloud PC roles, build a pilot flow, document preview status.

checks.name: Windows 365 Boot Configuration
checks.description: Windows 365 Boot turns a physical Windows endpoint into a thin client that signs the user directly into a Cloud PC at power-on, bypassing the local desktop. Verify whether Boot is configured for shared/kiosk personas and physical device access is correctly scoped.
checks.bestPractice: For shared workstation, kiosk, or branch personas, deploy Windows 365 Boot via the guided scenario in Intune. Restrict physical-device access using the dedicated Allow/Restrict policies.
checks.impact: Without Boot, shared physical devices keep a local profile surface that can drift, store local data, and complicate compliance.
checks.remediation: Run the Windows 365 Boot guided scenario in Intune. Validate physical device requirements (TPM, supported OS), assign the dedicated Cloud PC, and apply the Allow/Restrict access policy.

checks.name: Windows 365 Government Tenant Posture
checks.description: Windows 365 Government is a separate offering with its own URLs, regions, and compliance footprint (US Gov / DoD). Confirm whether the tenant is Government and, if so, that the configuration follows the Government setup guidance.
checks.bestPractice: For US public-sector workloads, deploy on Windows 365 Government, follow the documented tenant setup, and use Government-specific URLs/endpoints.
checks.impact: Running regulated workloads on the commercial offering may breach compliance commitments (FedRAMP High, IL5).
checks.remediation: Confirm tenant type. If Government is required, follow the 'Set up tenants for Windows 365 Government' procedure and re-baseline all checks against Government endpoints.

checks.name: Enrollment Status Page (ESP) for Cloud PCs
checks.description: The Enrollment Status Page (ESP) blocks the user from reaching the desktop until required apps and policies have applied. For Cloud PCs this prevents users landing on a half-configured PC.
checks.bestPractice: Enable ESP for Cloud PC enrollments with a curated list of blocking apps and a sane timeout (e.g. 60 minutes). Allow users to retry on failure.
checks.impact: Without ESP, users sign into a partially configured Cloud PC, generate help-desk tickets, and may bypass critical security tooling.
checks.remediation: Configure an Enrollment Status Page profile in Intune scoped to Cloud PCs. Define blocking apps, timeout, and failure behaviour. Pilot then enforce.

checks.name: Autopilot Device Preparation for Frontline
checks.description: Autopilot device preparation for Frontline streamlines first-run readiness on shared Frontline Cloud PCs so that target apps and policies are present before the next user signs in.
checks.bestPractice: Configure Autopilot device preparation for all Frontline shared provisioning policies. Track preparation outcomes via Intune reporting.
checks.impact: Without device preparation, Frontline shared sessions risk being assigned to a Cloud PC that is still applying configuration, leading to user-visible failures.
checks.remediation: Build the device-preparation profile for the Frontline persona, attach it to the provisioning policy, and validate end-to-end.

checks.name: Nested Virtualization Posture
checks.description: Some SKUs and scenarios (e.g. WSL2, Hyper-V containers, dev workloads) require nested virtualization. Confirm whether it is needed and that the chosen Cloud PC SKU/region supports it.
checks.bestPractice: Document whether developer or container personas require nested virtualization. If yes, pin them to SKUs and regions where it is supported and test before broad rollout.
checks.impact: Personas needing WSL2 / Hyper-V containers without nested virt support cannot run their tooling, blocking productivity.
checks.remediation: Identify in-scope personas. Confirm SKU/region support for nested virtualization. Validate WSL2 / Docker workloads on the chosen SKU.

checks.name: App Assure Engagement
checks.description: App Assure is a Microsoft program that helps remediate app-compatibility issues at no extra cost when moving apps to Windows 365 / Windows 11. Confirm engagement for migration projects.
checks.bestPractice: For any net-new migration of legacy line-of-business apps to Cloud PCs, raise an App Assure case to validate compatibility before broad rollout.
checks.impact: Without App Assure, in-house teams absorb the full cost of remediating app-compat issues that Microsoft would otherwise help fix at no charge.
checks.remediation: Identify migration apps in scope. Open an App Assure request via Microsoft FastTrack. Track findings into the project plan.

checks.name: Idle Session Time Limits
checks.description: Idle session time limits sign idle users out of their Cloud PC after a defined period, releasing concurrency on Frontline and reducing exposure if a session is left unattended.
checks.bestPractice: Set idle session time limits per persona (e.g. 60 minutes for Enterprise, shorter for shared Frontline) and align with the wider session-lock policy (W365-IAM-007).
checks.impact: Without idle limits, abandoned sessions occupy Frontline concurrency and increase data-leak exposure on the local endpoint.
checks.remediation: Configure idle session time limits via Intune Settings Catalog. Scope by persona group and validate on a pilot user.

checks.name: Screen Capture Protection
checks.description: Screen Capture Protection blocks the host OS from capturing the Cloud PC session window via screenshot or screen-recording tools, complementing watermarking.
checks.bestPractice: Enable Screen Capture Protection for data-sensitive personas in addition to watermarking. Communicate the change to end users to set expectations.
checks.impact: Without Screen Capture Protection, sensitive data displayed in the Cloud PC can be exfiltrated by trivial host-side screen-capture tools.
checks.remediation: Enable the Screen Capture Protection policy via Intune Settings Catalog and assign to in-scope groups. Validate that screenshot tools no longer capture session content.

checks.name: Share Restore Points to Storage Account
checks.description: Restore points can be shared to an Azure Storage account so a Cloud PC's disk can be inspected, recovered, or migrated outside the standard restore flow.
checks.bestPractice: Pre-configure a hardened storage account for sharing restore points and document the procedure as part of the IR / forensics runbook.
checks.impact: Without a documented procedure, urgent disk recovery or forensic evidence collection is delayed by ad-hoc storage provisioning.
checks.remediation: Provision a dedicated locked-down storage account (private endpoint, immutable blobs where possible). Document the share procedure and rehearse annually.

checks.name: Azure Firewall / NVA Egress Posture
checks.description: When Cloud PC traffic is forced through Azure Firewall or a third-party NVA, the firewall must allow the documented Windows 365 service URLs/IPs and avoid TLS-decrypting them.
checks.bestPractice: Use the documented FQDN tag / service tag in Azure Firewall to permit Windows 365 traffic. Exclude Cloud PC URLs from TLS inspection. Review rules quarterly.
checks.impact: Misconfigured egress firewall causes intermittent provisioning and connection failures and is a top root cause for Cloud PC outages.
checks.remediation: Apply the Windows 365 FQDN tag in Azure Firewall, exclude the URLs from TLS inspection, and verify allowance with the connection health check.

checks.name: Extended Security Updates (ESU) Posture
checks.description: Cloud PCs running Windows 10 require Extended Security Updates after the Win10 end-of-support date. Confirm whether ESU is licensed and applied where Win10 images are still in use.
checks.bestPractice: Migrate Win10 Cloud PCs to Win11 before EOS. Where migration is not possible, license ESU and confirm updates are flowing via Windows Update for Business / Autopatch.
checks.impact: Without ESU, Win10 Cloud PCs stop receiving security updates and become a ransomware/blast-radius risk inside the tenant.
checks.remediation: Identify Win10 Cloud PCs. Plan and execute Win11 migration. For any residual Win10, procure ESU keys and confirm patch compliance in the Windows Update report.

checks.name: Migration to Windows 365 Plan
checks.description: A documented migration plan is required when moving from on-prem VDI, AVD, or physical PCs to Windows 365 (personas, dependencies, comms, fallback).
checks.bestPractice: Build a migration plan covering persona inventory, app dependencies (App Assure), profile migration (FSLogix → OneDrive KFM), comms, training, and rollback windows.
checks.impact: Unplanned migrations stall mid-flight, leak budget, and erode user confidence in the platform.
checks.remediation: Use the Microsoft migration guide as a template. Define waves, dependencies, success criteria, and an exec dashboard. Tie into App Assure and FastTrack where eligible.

checks.name: Configuration Manager Co-Management Posture
checks.description: Cloud PCs can be co-managed by Microsoft Configuration Manager and Intune. Where ConfigMgr is in scope, the workload-slider configuration determines which authority owns each policy area and must be explicit to avoid conflicts.
checks.bestPractice: Decide whether ConfigMgr is in scope for Cloud PCs. If yes, document the workload-slider position per workload and pin Cloud PC enrollment to the Intune authority; otherwise leave Cloud PCs Intune-only.
checks.impact: Unmanaged co-management drift causes silent policy conflicts (compliance, app deployment, Windows Update) that are hard to triage in support.
checks.remediation: Document the co-management posture. For each workload, confirm the slider position, validate on a pilot Cloud PC, and add the configuration to the change-management baseline.

checks.name: Resource Performance Report Review
checks.description: The Cloud PC resource performance report tracks CPU, memory, and disk pressure across Cloud PCs and is the primary signal for sizing and saturation issues.
checks.bestPractice: Review the resource performance report monthly. Flag personas where CPU or memory pressure exceeds the thresholds for sustained periods and feed the findings into the right-sizing review (W365-COST-002).
checks.impact: Without periodic review, undersized Cloud PCs degrade user experience while oversized ones inflate cost; both are detectable from this report.
checks.remediation: Add the resource performance report to the monthly operational rhythm. Capture sizing decisions in change management.

checks.name: Windows Updates for Frontline Cloud PCs
checks.description: Windows 365 Frontline Cloud PCs are non-persistent in shared mode and require a different patching approach than Enterprise Cloud PCs. Update rings and Autopatch behaviour must be tuned for the Frontline lifecycle.
checks.bestPractice: For Frontline, drive update compliance via the image refresh cadence rather than per-device update rings. Schedule periodic image rebuilds and bulk reprovision to deliver patches deterministically.
checks.impact: Treating Frontline like Enterprise for updates causes patches to be lost on reprovision and creates a perpetual non-compliance signal in reporting.
checks.remediation: Define a Frontline patching strategy in the runbook: cadence for image rebuild, mapping to bulk reprovision (W365-USER-006), and reporting basis. Validate compliance via the Cloud PC inventory after each rebuild.

checks.name: Run BaselinePilot to Validate Cloud PC OS Baseline
checks.description: BaselinePilot (sister tool in this toolkit) audits a live Windows 11 Cloud PC against the Microsoft Security Baseline (Intune MDM 24H2 + GPO SCT 25H2) and operational health controls. Running BaselinePilot inside a representative Cloud PC validates that the baseline declared in Intune (W365-SEC-003) is actually applied at the OS layer, closing the gap between policy intent and effective configuration.
checks.bestPractice: For each provisioning policy / persona, sign in to a representative Cloud PC, run BaselinePilot, and archive the resulting HTML report alongside this assessment as evidence of baseline conformance. Re-run after every image refresh or significant policy change.
checks.impact: Without an OS-layer audit, baseline drift goes undetected: a Cloud PC may show as compliant in Intune yet still have insecure local settings, missing Defender exclusions, broken update channels, or stale registry policies from a previous image.
checks.remediation: Connect to one Cloud PC per persona. Launch BaselinePilot from the toolkit (scripts\BaselineAssessor\BaselinePilot.ps1), run the full collection, review failed/warning items, and feed remediation back into the Intune baseline or image build. Attach the BaselinePilot HTML report to the W365 assessment evidence pack.
